steps.title: Identify the logical relationship
steps.content: The logical relationship is sequence. The passage explicitly names three steps, marks step one with "First" and step three with "Third," so the blank needs a transition signaling the second item in an ordered list.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — "For example": signals exemplification, treating what follows as a specific instance of a general claim. Forming a hypothesis is not an example of researching a question. ✗
  • Choice B — "Second": signals the next item in a numbered sequence, completing the First → Second → Third structure the passage establishes, so fits. ✓
  • Choice C — "Therefore": signals cause-effect, implying forming a hypothesis is a logical consequence of researching. But the passage frames these as distinct steps, not cause and effect. ✗
  • Choice D — "Instead": signals contrast or replacement, implying forming a hypothesis replaces or contradicts the prior step. That reverses the intended meaning. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
